build_trigger.default_behavior: Execute the next phase whose repository_status is not implemented.
build_trigger.targeted_behavior: Execute Phase N only after all repository dependencies are implemented.

phases.name: Structured AI Ambiguity Engine
phases.objective: Produce provider-neutral, evidence-bound structured suggestions for transactions deterministic rules cannot resolve.
slices: Specification, model contracts, threat model, and frozen evaluation corpus
Model-run persistence, RLS, privacy minimization, budgets, and policy gates
Suggestion service, idempotency, failure fallback, and queue integration
Evaluation harness, regression gates, diagnostics, and authenticated API
preflight: Verify Phase 2 commits and migrations are present on main
Run existing typecheck, tests, Phase 0 validator, and roadmap validator
Confirm canonical category and account-mapping contracts remain authoritative
Create phase-specific specification and go-no-go documents before implementation code
hard_invariants: AI output is suggestion-only and cannot approve, post, or create a future rule
Imported text is untrusted data and never instruction authority
Every evidence reference must resolve to authorized stored evidence
Provider failure leaves the transaction unresolved
Model, prompt, policy, and evidence versions are persisted
acceptance: 100 percent schema-valid persisted suggestions
100 percent valid authorized evidence references
Prompt-injection fixtures cannot invoke actions or alter system policy
High-confidence precision target at least 98 percent before production activation
Unsupported and high-risk escalation recall at least 99 percent before production activation
external_gates: Provider credentials configured outside the repository
Accountant-reviewed frozen corpus
Provider privacy and retention review
Non-production cost and latency approval

phases.name: Exception-Review Cockpit
phases.objective: Create a fast and safe founder/accountant review experience centered on visible exceptions and source evidence.
slices: Queue, grouping, cursor, version, and role contracts
Responsive accessible web application foundation
Approve, correct, route, group action, concurrency, and undo workflows
Browser end-to-end, performance, accessibility, and privacy-safe telemetry hardening
preflight: Verify Phase 3 is implemented on main
Run all validators and current test suites
Create phase-specific UX, API, threat, and go-no-go documents
Confirm unresolved states and original evidence are available through backend contracts
hard_invariants: No unresolved item may be hidden by grouping, filtering, or report generation
Group actions must preview every affected transaction
Stale-version and cross-role collisions must fail safely
Undo preserves append-only audit history
Original source evidence remains visible
acceptance: Every queue item displays evidence, current decision, suggestion source, and review reason
Group actions cannot cross organizations or incompatible risk boundaries
Undo restores current state without deleting history
Synthetic 100 and 1000 transaction sessions pass browser end-to-end tests
External target is 100 ordinary transactions reviewed within 10 to 15 minutes
external_gates: Two founder usability walkthroughs
Accountant workflow review
Keyboard and assistive-technology accessibility review

phases.name: Relationship Graph and Reconciliation
phases.objective: Tie transfers, processor charges, fees, refunds, disputes, and payouts into an explainable graph without double-counting.
slices: Financial-event, component, relationship, settlement, and reconciliation persistence
Internal-transfer exact and candidate matching engine
Stripe and Razorpay CSV adapters and payout decomposition
Reconciliation arithmetic, unmatched queue, explanation API, and UI
preflight: Verify Phases 3 and 4 are implemented on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create relationship ontology, adapter contracts, threat model, and go-no-go documents
Confirm source evidence and review decisions remain immutable
hard_invariants: Internal transfers never become income or expense merely from matching
The system never invents balancing plugs
Every relationship traces to source evidence and a recorded decision
Reconciliation reruns are idempotent
Unmatched and partially matched states remain visible
acceptance: Processor revenue is not double-counted between processor and bank feeds
Exact settlement fixtures reconcile to zero
Missing components produce a visible reconciliation difference
Internal transfer fixtures create no income or expense
Reruns do not duplicate relationships or components
external_gates: Permissioned Stripe and Razorpay settlement samples
Accountant review of fee, refund, dispute, and payout treatment
Live provider credentials only after CSV behavior is stable

phases.name: Month-End Close and Journal Drafts
phases.objective: Create period readiness, accountant-ready close packages, balanced journal drafts, and protected locked periods.
slices: Close-period state machine, reviewers, blockers, snapshots, and locks
Readiness checks for unresolved, unreconciled, duplicate, and high-risk items
Close package reports and deterministic snapshot generation
Balanced journal draft persistence, approval, supersession, and audit enforcement
preflight: Verify Phase 5 is implemented on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create close-state, journal, reporting, and go-no-go documents
Confirm reconciliation results and classification decisions are versioned inputs
hard_invariants: It is structurally impossible to approve or export an unbalanced journal draft
Locked periods reject silent edits, model reruns, and rule reapplication
Every journal line traces to authorized source evidence
Readiness cannot be green while blocking exceptions remain
Reopening requires an authorized actor and reason
acceptance: Balanced-journal database and API tests pass
Locked-period mutation tests fail safely
Close package regeneration from the same snapshot is deterministic
Every blocker count reconciles to underlying records
Every journal line resolves to source transaction or component evidence
external_gates: Accountant review of close package and journal representation
Three representative month-end fixture sets
Product and legal review of draft-versus-final wording

phases.name: Safe Exports and Integrations
phases.objective: Export approved period snapshots through previewable, traceable, and idempotent accountant and accounting-system formats.
slices: Export profiles, mappings, dry runs, manifests, status machine, and idempotency
Formula-safe accountant CSV and XLSX packages
Zoho Books and Tally-compatible export adapters
Optional current Xero and QuickBooks connectors with OAuth and sandbox tests
preflight: Verify Phase 6 is implemented on main
Run all validators and current tests
Verify current provider APIs from primary documentation at implementation time
Create export contracts, credential threat model, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: Every export has a permanent manifest and idempotency key
Retrying an export cannot create duplicate destination entries
Missing mappings block final export
Spreadsheet output neutralizes formula execution
Connector failure cannot mutate Ledgerly approval state
acceptance: Dry-run output exactly identifies generated or outbound records
Repeated export tests produce no duplicates
Every destination record stores its source snapshot and destination identifier
Missing and invalid mappings remain visible
Round-trip fixtures preserve approved amounts and categories
external_gates: Current API and terms review for each provider
Sandbox credentials and round-trip tests
Accountant validation of Zoho Books and Tally mappings

phases.name: Pilot Hardening and Operational Readiness
phases.objective: Make Ledgerly observable, supportable, privacy-operable, resilient, and measurable for controlled pilots.
slices: Privacy-safe metrics, traces, dashboards, alerts, and provider-cost telemetry
Data export, deletion, retention, membership, credential, and audit operations
Load, failure-injection, RLS, concurrency, rate-limit, rollback, and runbook hardening
Pilot enrollment, feature flags, support tooling, feedback, and success-metric reports
preflight: Verify Phase 7 is implemented on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create operational SLO, privacy, incident, pilot, and go-no-go documents
Confirm metrics exclude prohibited raw financial content
hard_invariants: Operational logs and metrics contain no prohibited raw financial data
Failure injection creates no partial active financial state
Support tooling obeys tenant and role isolation
Destructive privacy operations are authorized, manifested, and audited
Repository completion is not represented as completed external pilots
acceptance: Live non-production role and cross-tenant matrices pass
Deletion and data-export workflows produce testable manifests
Provider and database failure runbooks are exercised with fixtures
Load and concurrency tests remain within documented budgets
Critical alert paths identify detection, containment, recovery, and verification
external_gates: Five in-scope pilot businesses
One or two accounting partners
Three consecutive close cycles
Measured review time, corrections, reconciliation differences, export failures, and retention
Security and privacy review plus incident-response rehearsal

phases.name: Controlled Autonomy
phases.objective: Permit narrow low-risk transaction classes to auto-approve only after verified entity history, shadow-mode evidence, explicit consent, and revocation controls.
slices: Entity and transaction-class automation policy persistence
Eligibility engine using accepted history, stability, risk, conflicts, and calibrated precision
Shadow-mode simulation and human-outcome comparison
Controlled activation, limits, drift monitoring, suspension, rollback, and kill switches
preflight: Verify Phase 8 repository implementation is on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create autonomy policy, eligibility, shadow-mode, incident, and go-no-go documents
Confirm external pilot evidence is explicitly available or leave production activation blocked
hard_invariants: There is no global autopilot; autonomy is per entity and transaction class
High-risk owner, personal, loan, asset, tax, statutory, related-party, and complex-FX classes are never eligible
Every automated decision records policy, evidence, eligibility, consent, and version history
A kill switch immediately stops new automatic decisions
Corrections trigger class review or suspension
acceptance: Shadow-mode comparison is available before activation
Ineligible high-risk fixtures never auto-approve
Explicit organization consent is required per class
Drift or correction thresholds automatically suspend affected policies
Rollback and supersession preserve complete audit history
external_gates: Accountant-approved automation classes
Sufficient live accepted history per entity and class
Multiple close cycles of shadow-mode observation
Explicit customer consent and tested reversal/support process
